concepts.title: Group 0
concepts.conceptBody:
Group zero: $\mathbf{(NH}_{4}^{+}\mathbf{)}$

  • Salt$+\text{NaOH}\xrightarrow{\Delta }$ gas giving white fumes with HCl

$\text{N}{{\text{H}}_{4}}\text{Cl}+\text{NaOH}\xrightarrow{\Delta }\text{N}{{\text{H}}_{3}}(~\text{g})+\text{NaCl}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}$

$\text{N}{{\text{H}}_{3}}(~\text{g})+\text{HCl}\xrightarrow{{}}\underset{white\text{ }fumes}{\mathop{\text{N}{{\text{H}}_{4}}\text{Cl}}}\,$

$\text{N}{{\text{H}}_{4}}\text{Cl}+\text{NaOH}\xrightarrow{\Delta }\text{N}{{\text{H}}_{3}}$

Solubility Trends

dil. $\text{HN}{{\text{O}}_{3}}$ $\text{N}{{\text{H}}_{4}}\text{OH}$ Hypo soln $\text{C}{{\text{N}}^{-}}$soln.

1. \(\begin{array}{lcccc} & \text { dil. } \mathrm{HNO}_3 & \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH} & \text { Hypo soln } & \mathrm{CN}^{-} \text {soln. } \\ \mathrm{AgCl} & & 3 & 3 & 3 \\ \mathrm{AgBr} & & 3 & 3 & 3 \\ \mathrm{AgI} & & & 3 & 3 \\ \mathrm{Ag}_2 \mathrm{~S} & 3 & & 3 & 3
\end{array}\)

2. All water insoluble black metal sulphide are soluble in hot and dil. $\text{HN}{{\text{O}}_{3}}$ except $\text{HgS}$. (Soluble in aqua regia)

3. FeS is the only black metal sulphide soluble in non- oxidising acid.

4. In general $\text{Pb}$ salts are soluble in dil. $\text{HN}{{\text{O}}_{3}}$ & Ex. $\text{NaOH}$ Soln.

5. All water insoluble ${{\text{C}}_{2}}\text{O}_{4}^{2-}$ salts are insoluble in acetic acid except $\text{Ba}{{\text{C}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{4}}$ (white).
